subject: Mira Tan (fictional)
corpus: Notes on Friction — invented essay series, ~80 essays (does not exist)
disclaimer: Fabricated for illustration. No real essays, no real person.

repeated_core_claims.claim: Friction is a feature you didn't choose
repeated_core_claims.gloss: Every extra tap, field, or confirm is a design decision — usually an unmade one. Audit a flow by counting the friction nobody decided to add.
evidence: essay 'The Tax You Forgot to Levy'
essay 'The Confirm Dialog Is an Apology'

repeated_core_claims.claim: The second draft is the real first draft
repeated_core_claims.gloss: Nothing is right in v1; design the system so the cheap rewrite happens on purpose, not the expensive one later.
The default move on any flow is removal; adding is the exception that has to argue for itself.
evidence: essay 'The Subtraction Bias We Don't Have'
essay 'Every Setting Is a Lost Argument'

repeated_core_claims.gloss: The empty/zero-data state is where a product is honest about its one job; design it before the happy path.

coined_terms.meaning: walk a flow counting every unchosen point of resistance
the single task a screen exists to get done; if you can't name it, the screen is wrong
every toggle added because the team couldn't agree, paid back as user confusion

reading_list_influences: an austere industrial-design lineage — 'subtract until it hurts' (a fictional, invented influence)
early usability writing on think-aloud testing
restaurant kitchen mise-en-place writing (she cites cooking constantly)
contradictions_noted: Preaches 'subtract before you add', yet essay 'The Setting I Kept' defends one hard-won toggle — subtraction is a default, not an absolute.
source: huashu-nuwa Phase 1 Agent 1 著作; bar = core claims repeated >=3x are true beliefs
